header.tagline: Audio Cue Playback for Live Events
header.screenshotAlt: LivePlay main interface showing the playlist editor, cue cart, and properties panel

download.title: Download LivePlay
download.subtitle: Free and open-source. Available for Windows, macOS, and Linux.

macGatekeeper.title: macOS: "LivePlay is damaged and can't be opened"?
macGatekeeper.intro: LivePlay is not yet signed with an Apple Developer certificate, so macOS quarantines it on download. The app is safe — this just means Apple hasn't been paid to notarize it. To open it, remove the quarantine flag once after installing to /Applications:
macGatekeeper.outro: Enter your password when prompted, then launch LivePlay normally. You only need to do this once per install.

winSmartScreen.title: Windows: "Windows protected your PC"?
winSmartScreen.intro: LivePlay isn't yet signed with a certificate Microsoft SmartScreen recognises, so Windows may show a blue "Windows protected your PC" warning when you run the installer. The app is safe — it simply hasn't built up download reputation yet (code signing is on the way).
winSmartScreen.step1: Click "More info" on the warning dialog.
winSmartScreen.step2: Click the "Run anyway" button that appears, then continue the installation normally.
winSmartScreen.outro: If your browser blocked the download instead, choose "Keep" to save the installer first.

contribute.title: Contribute
contribute.subtitle: LivePlay is free and open-source — and always will be.
contribute.freeText: LivePlay is completely free to download and use — no licence fees, no subscriptions, no paywalls. Every feature is available to everyone, with no premium tiers.
contribute.licenseText: LivePlay is released under the GNU AGPL-3.0 licence. In plain terms: you are free to use, study, share and modify it. If you share a modified version — or run it as a network service — you must make your source code available under the same licence, so the whole community keeps benefiting.
contribute.contributeText: Anyone can contribute. Report bugs, suggest features, improve translations or submit code — every contribution is welcome. Head to the GitHub repository to open an issue or a pull request.

interface.title: Intuitive Interface
interface.description: LivePlay features a clean, simple and modern interface designed specifically for live sound operators. Organize your main sound files in playlists, and add sounds to a cart player for quick access or announcements. Audio files added to the cart will automatically behave as announcements, ducking any background audio when played.

audioEngine.title: Robust C++ Audio Engine
audioEngine.description: At the heart of LivePlay is a dedicated, high-performance audio engine written in C++ that talks directly to your sound hardware — WASAPI on Windows, Core Audio on macOS, and ALSA on Linux. Every cue runs as a fully independent playback instance with its own decoder and fade envelope, so playing the same file twice at different volumes always behaves exactly as expected. The engine runs as a standalone server process: it can live inside the desktop app, or run headless on a dedicated backstage machine wired to your audio interfaces.

limiter.title: Brick-Wall Limiter & Loudness Targets
limiter.description: A true brick-wall limiter sits on every output channel, keeping your levels consistent and protecting your hardware and audience from runaway peaks. Choose from built-in output targets that match professional loudness standards — EBU R128, streaming, radio, Netflix and live — and LivePlay applies the right ceiling, metering zones and waveform calibration automatically.

meters.title: Broadcast-Grade Metering
meters.description: Trust what you hear with real-time, broadcast-quality metering at every stage of the signal path — per-cue, per mixer channel, and on the master output. Colour-coded meter zones follow the loudness target you've selected, so you can see at a glance whether you're sitting comfortably within spec or pushing into the red.

routing.title: Multi-Device Audio Routing
routing.description: Route audio from any cue to any combination of output channels across multiple sound cards simultaneously, with independent output assignment per item. The routing matrix lets you split a stereo file into individual channels and send each one to a different device with its own gain — perfect for feeding front-of-house, a monitor mix, and a recording output from a single cue, all at once.

previewLtc.title: Dedicated Preview & LTC Timecode
previewLtc.description: Audition cues silently on a dedicated preview device — headphones or a cue bus — without ever touching your main outputs, so you can line up the next cue while the show rolls on. A separate SMPTE LTC timecode generator outputs a continuous linear timecode signal on its own channel, letting you synchronise lighting desks, video playback and other external devices to LivePlay.

waveform.title: Waveform Trimmer
waveform.description: Trim your audio with precision using the built-in waveform editor. Set in and out points, adjust volume, and configure fade times visually. You can also automatically trim based on silence, or automatically adjust the volume so that all audio levels are consistent.

youtube.title: YouTube Integration
youtube.description: Download audio directly from YouTube with our built-in downloader! Perfect for quickly adding music, sound effects, or backing tracks to your show (please make sure you have the rights to use the content or are using public domain content).

properties.title: Easy to use Properties Panel
properties.description: Change the name, color, and playback properties of your audio files with ease. Set files to loop, adjust their volume, start and end behaviours, and configure automatic silence trimming all from one convenient location. You are in control of your show's flow!

ducking.title: Automatic Ducking
ducking.description: Configure automatic ducking to lower the volume of background music when announcements or dialogue play. Perfect for smooth transitions in live productions.

discovery.title: Automatic Discovery & Rock-Solid Reliability
discovery.description: LivePlay servers announce themselves on your local network, so clients find them automatically — no IP addresses to type in. If the audio engine ever stops unexpectedly, it relaunches itself instantly and resumes playback right where it left off, so your show keeps running. And because the engine is a true server, multiple clients can connect to the same instance at once — ideal for a primary operator and a backup, or a stage manager monitoring playback alongside the sound op.

remoteOperation.title: Local or Remote Operation
remoteOperation.description: The LivePlay audio server launches automatically alongside the application, or can be run on a dedicated backstage machine with your audio interfaces plugged in. Connect from any computer on the same network and take full control — transport, properties, routing, and live metering all work remotely, giving you the flexibility to separate your control position from your audio hardware.

firstLaunch.title: Quick Setup
firstLaunch.description: Get started in seconds with LivePlay's streamlined setup process. Select a folder, create a project and start importing files! All your media gets automatically collected into one place so you can move your project between computers with ease.

ports.title: Network Ports
ports.subtitle: If you're running the server and client on different machines, make sure these ports are open on your network and firewall.

control.title: Control Surface
control.description: REST API and WebSocket connection carrying transport commands, project data, routing and live meters between the client and server.

discovery.title: Automatic Discovery
discovery.description: Broadcast and multicast beacon (group 239.255.69.80) that lets clients find LivePlay servers on the local network automatically.

ports.note: A single-machine install needs no configuration — the client and server talk over 127.0.0.1 and the Windows installer adds the firewall rules for you. These ports only matter when separating the control position from the audio hardware across a LAN.
